sections.content:
Now the canvas shows:
- Rows 34-38: all purple (top half)
- Rows 39-43: all white (bottom half)
This is purple on top, white on bottom - the OPPOSITE of the target! The target (reference panel) shows white on top, purple on bottom.
So clicking on the bottom half of the canvas (row 41) with purple selected painted the TOP half purple. That's confusing. Let me think about this...
Wait, maybe the click position determines which half gets painted, but in an inverted way? Or maybe the click position determines the "anchor" and the paint fills the opposite side?
Actually, looking at the initial successful sequence:
1. MOUSE(row=28, col=31) - clicked ABOVE the canvas
2. MOUSE(row=38, col=31) - clicked ON the canvas (middle)
3. SPACE - painted top half purple
And now:
1. MOUSE(row=4, col=45) - clicked on purple bucket
2. MOUSE(row=41, col=31) - clicked on bottom half of canvas
3. SPACE - painted top half purple
Both times the top half got painted purple. Maybe the mechanic is:
- Select a color (by clicking the bucket)
- Click on the canvas to set the "cursor" position
- SPACE paints from the cursor position in some direction
Or maybe SPACE always paints the top half with the selected color, regardless of where you click?
Let me try a different approach. What if I select white and paint, then select purple and paint? The canvas is currently purple on top, white on bottom. If I select white and paint, maybe it'll paint the top half white, giving me white on top, purple on bottom.
Let me try: click white bucket, click canvas, SPACE.
